diet_advice.current_diet_assessment: Consistent high intake of sugary drinks and junk food significantly undermines efforts for a low-carbohydrate diet and weight management.
priority_changes: Strictly eliminate all soft drinks and packaged snacks from your diet.
Prioritize protein and non-starchy vegetables as the main components of every meal.
Increase intake of healthy fats through nuts/seeds and lean protein sources to improve HDL.
diet_advice.breakfast: Two boiled eggs with a handful of peanuts and 1 small bowl of chopped cucumber/tomato. — Provides high protein, healthy fats, and fiber with minimal carbohydrates.
diet_advice.lunch: Fish or chicken curry (with plenty of mixed vegetables like drumsticks, gourds) and 1 small bowl of plain curd. — Rich in lean protein, essential fats, and fiber from vegetables, fitting a low-carb approach.
diet_advice.dinner: A large bowl of mixed vegetable soup with shredded paneer (cottage cheese). — Light, satisfying, high in fiber and protein, and very low in carbohydrates.
foods_to_avoid: Soft drinks — directly contribute to high blood sugar and impede weight loss.
Chips, biscuits, samosas — high in refined carbohydrates, unhealthy fats, and empty calories.
White rice, idli, dosa, paratha — rapidly raise blood glucose due to their high carbohydrate content.
diet_advice.clinical_note: Critically low HDL cholesterol (35.5 mg/dL) and persistent poor sleep duration (4 hours) require urgent medical attention and targeted lifestyle interventions.
